goal: Build a serverless web platform that accepts Word/Draw.io submissions, extracts grading evidence, generates criterion-level AI scoring suggestions, and lets lecturers approve final grades.

global_context.framework: React + Vite + TypeScript, Supabase, Supabase Edge Functions
global_context.architecture: serverless web app with Supabase Auth, Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, asynchronous grading jobs, and OpenRouter AI integration
constraints: AI suggestions must never overwrite lecturer-approved final scores
Student-visible grades require lecturer publication in MVP
Rubric templates are uploaded as constrained .docx files with standardized tables
Student submissions require one .docx report and one .drawio diagram
Store AI suggestions, final lecturer scores, extraction artifacts, and audit events separately
Keep grading states explicit: uploaded, extracting, extracted, grading, graded, reviewed, published, failed

steps.description: Scaffold the React/Vite TypeScript application, shared lint/build configuration, environment variable examples, and baseline project documentation.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created React/Vite TypeScript scaffold, environment example, baseline README, and initial landing workflow UI.
debug_logs: npm.cmd install completed and generated package-lock.json.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully.
npm audit reported 5 vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies; no force fix was applied.
success_criteria: The project installs dependencies from package.json
npm run build completes successfully
.env.example documents Supabase and OpenRouter configuration keys

steps.description: Create the Supabase schema for roles, subjects, assignments, rubrics, submissions, extracted artifacts, grading results, final approvals, audit events, and storage buckets.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created Supabase config, initial schema migration, storage bucket/policy migration, and TypeScript database shape definitions.
debug_logs: Schema separates ai_criterion_scores from final_grades and grade_publications.
RLS policies restrict unpublished final grades to lecturers; students can read final grades only after publication.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully after database types were added.
Supabase migrations were not applied to a live local database in this step.
success_criteria: Database tables separate AI suggested scores from lecturer final scores
Row-level security prevents students from reading unpublished grades
Storage policies restrict students to their own submission files and lecturers to authorized subjects

steps.description: Add shared TypeScript domain models, validation schemas, grading state constants, and helper utilities used by both frontend and Edge Functions.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created shared grading states, domain models, Zod validation schemas, and a typed Supabase client.
debug_logs: Initial build failed because import.meta.env needed Vite client types.
Added src/vite-env.d.ts.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully.
success_criteria: Validation schemas cover rubric rows, submissions, extraction artifacts, AI criterion scores, and final criterion scores
Score validation rejects suggested or final scores above each criterion max score
Frontend and backend code can import consistent grading state values

steps.description: Implement Edge Function extraction logic for rubric .docx tables, student .docx sections, and Draw.io XML nodes/connectors.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created Supabase Edge extraction function plus shared parsers for .docx text/tables, rubric tables, Draw.io XML, and storage/database helpers.
debug_logs: npm.cmd run build completed successfully after extraction files were added.
Edge Function Deno type-checking was not run because Supabase CLI/local Edge runtime was not invoked.
DOCX parsing uses OOXML document.xml extraction via fflate in the Edge runtime.
success_criteria: Rubric .docx parsing returns normalized criteria with identifiers, descriptions, max scores, grading guidance, and deduction notes
Student .docx parsing stores section text and extraction warnings
Draw.io parsing stores entities, labels, connectors, and relationships
Extraction failure updates the submission state to failed with a visible reason

steps.description: Implement the OpenRouter grading Edge Function that combines rubric criteria, extracted document text, diagram structure, and warnings into structured AI scoring results.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created OpenRouter grading Edge Function with structured prompt construction, AI JSON response parsing, score-bound validation, grading run persistence, and failure state handling.
debug_logs: npm.cmd run build completed successfully after AI grading files were added.
AI parser rejects unknown criterion IDs, missing criteria, and suggested scores above rubric max scores.
Edge Function Deno type-checking and live OpenRouter calls were not run in this step.
success_criteria: AI responses are parsed into criterion-level records containing max_score, suggested_score, deductions, evidence, comment, and confidence/status
Suggested scores are clamped or rejected if they exceed rubric max scores
Raw AI request/response metadata is stored for audit without exposing secrets
AI grading failure updates the submission state to failed with a visible reason

steps.description: Add frontend service modules for authentication, subject/rubric management, student submissions, grading job triggers, review saving, publication, and retries.
steps.ai_generated_code: Created frontend service modules for auth/profile, subjects/assignments, rubric upload, student submissions, extraction/grading triggers, review saving, publishing, and retries.
debug_logs: Initial build failed because manual Supabase types lacked Relationships metadata.
Added Relationships fields to database.types.ts.
Nested rubric_criteria select required a service-boundary cast in reviewService.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully.
success_criteria: Students can create a submission record and upload one .docx plus one .drawio file
Lecturers can upload a rubric .docx for a selected subject
Lecturers can save final criterion scores without mutating AI suggested scores
Lecturers can publish results only after final scores are present

steps.description: Build the application UI for role-aware navigation, lecturer subject/rubric setup, student upload flow, grading status, split-view lecturer review, and published student results.
steps.ai_generated_code: Built dashboard-oriented UI shell, file dropzones, status badge, lecturer dashboard, rubric upload, student submission, split review, and student result pages.
debug_logs: npm.cmd run build completed successfully after UI files were added.
UI currently uses local view switching and scaffold data; live Supabase wiring is planned in the integrate step.
success_criteria: Lecturer review page shows extracted evidence and editable criterion scores/comments in a split view
Student upload page accepts only .docx and .drawio files for the required slots
Students cannot see unpublished grades in the UI
All grading states render clear status and failure messages

steps.description: Add focused automated tests for parsers, validation, scoring invariants, service behavior, and key UI workflows.
steps.ai_generated_code: Added Vitest/jsdom setup plus parser, validation, review-service, and review-page tests.
debug_logs: Installed test dependencies: fflate, Testing Library, jest-dom, user-event, and jsdom.
Initial rubric parser tests found ambiguous Vietnamese header aliases.
Fixed rubric header matching by preferring exact aliases and separating title/description aliases.
npm.cmd test completed successfully: 6 test files, 10 tests.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully after tests were added.
success_criteria: Parser tests cover valid input, missing required sections, and malformed files
Validation tests prove scores cannot exceed criterion max scores
Review service tests prove final scores are stored separately from AI suggestions
npm test completes successfully

steps.description: Wire the end-to-end workflow from uploads through extraction, AI grading, lecturer review, final publication, retries, and visible audit/status updates.
steps.ai_generated_code: Added submission and review workflow orchestration modules and updated README with implemented status and local checks.
debug_logs: npm.cmd test completed successfully: 6 test files, 10 tests.
npm.cmd run build completed successfully.
Live Supabase end-to-end execution was not run because no local/remote Supabase project is configured in this workspace.
success_criteria: A lecturer can upload a rubric, a student can submit files, and a grading result can be reviewed and published in the local/dev environment
Retry controls can re-run extraction or AI grading before publication
Audit events are written for upload, extraction, AI grading, lecturer edit, and publication actions

steps.description: Document setup, environment variables, Supabase deployment, rubric .docx table format, submission template expectations, and known MVP limitations.
